title: Responsive site design system

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling draft for Responsive site design system; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: CSS Snapshot 2024; W3C Working Group Note 2024; provisions applicable to Responsive site design system
sources.claim: A CSS project system combines documented tokens, layers, primitives, components, responsive rules, states, and visual regression or manual checks.

sources.locator: Web Content Accessibility Guidelines 2.2; W3C Recommendation 2023; provisions applicable to Responsive site design system
sources.claim: A CSS project system combines documented tokens, layers, primitives, components, responsive rules, states, and visual regression or manual checks.

canonicalExplanation.summary: A CSS project system combines documented tokens, layers, primitives, components, responsive rules, states, and visual regression or manual checks.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A small site reuses spacing and color tokens while each component demonstrates default, focus, error, long-content, and narrow states. Your goal is to deliver and explain a responsive, accessible and maintainable CSS system with documented compatibility targets. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Responsive design and motion; Stylesheet organization; Compatibility and progressive enhancement; Visual accessibility audit; html project site. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: A CSS project system combines documented tokens, layers, primitives, components, responsive rules, states, and visual regression or manual checks. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: A token catalog can still be inconsistent, component variants can explode, and global rules can leak across independently authored pages. This draft therefore rejects: A design system is complete once colors and spacing variables are defined.

includes: Integrate tokens, layers, typography, layout, components, states and preferences across a multi-page site.
Deliver and explain a responsive, accessible and maintainable CSS system with documented compatibility targets.
excludes: A token catalog can still be inconsistent, component variants can explode, and global rules can leak across independently authored pages.

outcomes: Deliver and explain a responsive, accessible and maintainable CSS system with documented compatibility targets.

examples.title: Responsive site design system: concrete case
examples.situation: A small site reuses spacing and color tokens while each component demonstrates default, focus, error, long-content, and narrow states.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: A small site reuses spacing and color tokens while each component demonstrates default, focus, error, long-content, and narrow states.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: A CSS system also needs ownership, component contracts, state coverage, responsive behavior, accessibility checks, and change governance.
examples.result: The result follows because A CSS system also needs ownership, component contracts, state coverage, responsive behavior, accessibility checks, and change governance.

examples.title: Responsive site design system: boundary case
examples.situation: A token catalog can still be inconsistent, component variants can explode, and global rules can leak across independently authored pages.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Start from the documented boundary: A token catalog can still be inconsistent, component variants can explode, and global rules can leak across independently authored pages.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Reject the misconception: A design system is complete once colors and spacing variables are defined.
examples.result: The boundary requires this correction: A CSS system also needs ownership, component contracts, state coverage, responsive behavior, accessibility checks, and change governance.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: A design system is complete once colors and spacing variables are defined.
misconceptions.correction: A CSS system also needs ownership, component contracts, state coverage, responsive behavior, accessibility checks, and change governance.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Use a concrete CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling artifact to explain why this belief fails: A design system is complete once colors and spacing variables are defined.

analogy.example: A layered stage design can model Responsive site design system, where rules size, place, and paint existing content.
usefulFor: Building a first mental model for Responsive site design system before using exact platform terms
limitations: Physical layers do not reproduce cascade algorithms, intrinsic sizing, writing modes, or user preferences.
